# Part 23 — Miscellaneous Rules about Evidence

## PART 23 E+W MISCELLANEOUS RULES ABOUT EVIDENCE


### Scope and interpretation of this Part E+W


23.1. Rules 23.2 to 23.6 apply to evidence to which the Children (Admissibility of Hearsay Evidence) Order 1993 M1 does not apply.


Marginal Citations


M1 S.I. 1993/621 .


### Notice of intention to rely on hearsay evidence E+W


23.2. —(1) Where a party intends to rely on hearsay evidence at the final hearing and either—


that party complies with section 2(1)(a) of the Civil Evidence Act 1995 M2 by serving a witness statement on the other parties in accordance with the court's directions.


(2) Where paragraph (1)(b) applies, the party intending to rely on the hearsay evidence must, when serving the witness statement—


(3) In all other cases where a party intends to rely on hearsay evidence at the final hearing, that party complies with section 2(1)(a) of the Civil Evidence Act 1995 by serving a notice on the other parties which—


(4) The party proposing to rely on the hearsay evidence must—


Marginal Citations


M2 1995 c.38 .


### Circumstances in which notice of intention to rely on hearsay evidence is not required E+W


23.3. Section 2(1) of the Civil Evidence Act 1995 (duty to give notice of intention to rely on hearsay evidence) does not apply—


### Power to call witness for cross-examination on hearsay evidence E+W


23.4. —(1) Where a party—


the court may, on the application of any other party, permit that party to call the maker of the statement to be cross-examined (GL) on the contents of the statement.


(2) An application for permission to cross-examine (GL) under this rule must be made within 14 days beginning with the date on which a notice of intention to rely on the hearsay evidence was served on the applicant.
